defends: Email domain authentication: the sending domain publishes well-formed SPF, DKIM and DMARC so it cannot be spoofed for phishing (baseline: information transfer, A.5.14 / A.8.24).

context.verified: TE-15 verified DKIM/SPF/MX live on tessera-project.dev (Resend onboarding).
context.dns_caveat: The container's resolver is NOT the public internet. A live `--domain tessera-project.dev` run may return status=unknown in-container even though the records exist publicly (DNS resolution failure must be unknown, never a silent fail). Use `--resolver-fixture` for deterministic offline verification; the SAME parser runs on fixture and live data.

note: Fixed evidence manifest — sending domain and DKIM selectors are not model discretion (WORKING_METHOD §1). dkim_selectors is the set of selectors tried; a pass needs ANY ONE to resolve to a valid DKIM record.
